posts.text:
US President Trump on social media: 

“… the Hormuz Strait is OPEN, THE OIL IS GUSHING…”
posts.summary: Trump's statement that the Hormuz Strait is open and oil production is flowing suggests reassurance about the availability of crude supplies through a critical global chokepoint. The comment implies no immediate supply disruptions in a strategically important shipping route for oil exports.

posts.text: Talks between Iran and US in Switzerland have paused but not ended - Iranian source to Reuters
posts.summary: Negotiations between Iran and the US have temporarily paused in Switzerland, which could affect oil market stability given Iran's role as a major oil producer. The pause rather than cessation of talks suggests ongoing potential for diplomatic resolution that may impact future crude supply dynamics.

posts.text: Not saying IEA has been consistantly wrong in their demand forecasts for the last decade. But, 2025 "actual demand" has already surpassed their 2030 demand forecast. (2020 should be negated all together, no one could have forcasted that) https://t.co/2YmyGCQdNx
posts.summary: The IEA has significantly underestimated oil demand growth over the past decade, with 2025 actual demand already exceeding their 2030 forecast, suggesting their projections may continue to underestimate future demand trajectories in the oil market.

posts.text: Again it’s so important to always remember that prior to this war the Strait of Hormuz has never been closed and transit has always been free. https://t.co/COIsPoCdcv
posts.summary: Rory Johnston emphasizes that the Strait of Hormuz has maintained open transit before the current conflict, highlighting the historical significance of unobstructed passage through this critical waterway. The implication is that any disruption to this historically free transit would represent a notable shift with potential consequences for global oil markets.

posts.text: Together with impeded Strait of Hormuz, this sets up for a Sunday night/Monday morning rally in US gasoline and diesel prices. Time will tell whether these are transient events. https://t.co/0cUweluXrk
posts.summary: Tom Kloza anticipates rising US gasoline and diesel prices on Sunday night or Monday morning due to combined disruptions affecting the Strait of Hormuz and another unspecified factor, though he notes these impacts may be temporary.

Note to Subscribers - Newsletter Delivery

I have published the daily digest of Best in Energy news and research every business day since 2014 and my own newsletter roughly three times a week since late 2024. The only exceptions are public holidays and about five weeks of annual https://t.co/rtxGOzVzfA

There are no direct oil market implications stated in this announcement, which primarily addresses newsletter delivery consistency.

posts.text: Copper inventories monitored by the three major futures exchanges fell by 54.7 kt last week to 1.088 million tons, extending the recent drawdown trend and reinforcing signs of tightening availability outside the United States. The decline was led by the SHFE, where stocks dropped https://t.co/Bsa11N6g52
posts.summary: Copper inventories across major futures exchanges declined significantly last week, with Chinese SHFE stocks leading the drawdown and signaling tightening copper availability outside the U.S. This inventory reduction reinforces a broader trend of declining copper stocks globally.

posts.text:
Electricity is moving to the centre of Southeast Asia’s energy future, with demand already rising 2x as fast as overall energy use

Electric mobility in particular is growing fast – improving long-term energy security & bringing environmental gains 👉 https://t.co/kv0Ch9TCoO https://t.co/ykbzqbY1Mv
posts.summary: Electricity demand in Southeast Asia is growing twice as fast as overall energy demand, with rapid electric mobility expansion potentially reducing future oil consumption in the region.

posts.text: Over the past seven weeks, managed money's net long position in Brent crude has collapsed from a 7½-year high of 496k contracts to a 2026 low at 114k contracts in the reporting week ending 16 June, when prospects of a US-Iran peace deal gathered momentum and Brent subsequently https://t.co/IP1pJPCahF
posts.summary: Managed money investors dramatically reduced their net long positions in Brent crude oil over seven weeks, falling from a 7.5-year high of 496,000 contracts to a 2026 low of 114,000 contracts by mid-June, driven by growing prospects of a US-Iran peace deal.

posts.text:
DoC liquids supply market share to increase after non-DoC supply peaks in the 2030s.

By the 2030s, non-DoC supply is projected to peak and experience a long plateau at around 60 mb/d. Canada and Argentina stand out among non-DoC producers as countries where supply is forecast to https://t.co/jSGV7arPGa
posts.summary: OPEC expects non-member oil supply to peak in the 2030s at around 60 million barrels per day, which would subsequently increase OPEC's market share as non-member production plateaus. Canada and Argentina are highlighted as exceptions among non-OPEC producers where supply is forecast to continue growing.

posts.text: BREAKING: According to GasBuddy data, the U.S. average price of diesel has dropped to $4.99/gal, the first time it has fallen below $5 since March 15.
posts.summary: U.S. diesel prices have fallen below $5 per gallon for the first time since mid-March, according to GasBuddy data. The decline suggests easing pressure on fuel costs in the domestic market.

posts.text: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is now closed. The US says it remains open and that 17 million barrels of oil crossed the waterway today (that’s higher than pre-war, and a lot higher when the bypass pipelines volumes are added). https://t.co/LbkE3Iq9Ya
posts.summary: Iran claims the Strait of Hormuz is closed, but the US reports that oil transit through the waterway reached 17 million barrels today, exceeding pre-war levels even before accounting for bypass pipeline volumes. The conflicting narratives suggest minimal actual disruption to global oil flows despite the geopolitical tensions.

posts.text: Nationwide  gas average slips to $3.95 while diesel plummets to $5.062/gal. News of another Strait of Hormuz closure won't arrest further price drops this week, with plenty of "catching up" to previous  wholesale moves. But an on again/off again Strait looms for summer.
posts.summary: Gas prices are declining with the national average at $3.95 and diesel at $5.062 per gallon, with further drops expected this week despite potential Strait of Hormuz disruptions. The market faces ongoing uncertainty as intermittent closures of this critical shipping route could create volatility heading into summer.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/ODvGsvloiQ
posts.summary: Global energy spending is expected to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026, with approximately $1.2 trillion allocated to fossil fuels including oil, natural gas, and coal. This represents a smaller share compared to the $2.2 trillion directed toward clean energy infrastructure and technologies.

posts.text: Thirty million exported Iranian barrels later…. https://t.co/jzf8Ijzi2M

posts.text:
Global energy demand growth was met by diverse range of sources in 2025 – led by solar & then gas.

While growth slowed from the previous year against a complex economic and geopolitical backdrop, electricity use outpaced overall demand.

More 👉 https://t.co/c9Z4hPfenX https://t.co/q4qHxkQDGr
posts.summary: The IEA indicates that global energy demand growth in 2025 was led by solar and gas rather than oil, suggesting continued displacement of oil's market share as renewable and alternative sources expand. The slowdown in overall demand growth against economic and geopolitical challenges implies softer conditions for oil consumption.

posts.text: Iran isn’t losing any time re-starting its oil production and fresh exports (📷 via @CopernicusEU Sentinel-2 🛰️ from earlier today, showing multiple super tankers loading at Iran’s Kharg Island terminal) https://t.co/agqZcLzA5H
posts.summary: Iran is rapidly ramping up oil production and exports, with satellite imagery showing multiple supertankers loading at Kharg Island terminal, which could increase global oil supply.

posts.text:
Road transport, petrochemicals and aviation are central to future oil demand growth.

From a sectoral perspective, oil demand growth out to 2050 is set to be driven by road
transportation, aviation and petrochemicals, with increases of 5.7 mb/d, 4.2 mb/d and 4.6 mb/d, https://t.co/YSf1tTvs8v
posts.summary: OPEC projects that global oil demand through 2050 will be primarily driven by growth in road transportation, aviation, and petrochemicals, with combined increases totaling approximately 14.5 million barrels per day across these three sectors.

posts.text: Joint Maritime Information Center says oil tankers and other vessels can use the southern (Omani) shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz with “AIS on, radars radiating, running lights on, and normal use of VHF” either at night or day. https://t.co/f7Yyv9SIs5
posts.summary: The Joint Maritime Information Center has clarified that vessels can transit the southern Omani shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz safely while maintaining standard navigation practices, potentially reducing concerns about disruptions to oil shipments through this critical waterway.

posts.text: From New York to California, clean energy targets are being softened amid political and legal pressure. But with solar now among the cheapest sources of new power, critics argue the answer isn't weaker climate goals—it's faster permitting and more deployment. #CleanEnergy
posts.summary: Clean energy targets are facing pressure to weaken across major U.S. states, though solar's declining costs suggest faster deployment rather than reduced climate goals could address concerns. The shift toward cleaner power sources has potential implications for traditional oil demand as renewable energy becomes increasingly cost-competitive.

posts.text: For National Safety Month, we're spotlighting Senior Personal Safety Professional Fred Bowser. We asked Fred about his work and how the many safety professionals across Marathon Petroleum help uphold our culture of safety every day. 🔗 https://t.co/95fyj5izOU
posts.summary: Marathon Petroleum highlighted its safety culture during National Safety Month by featuring a senior safety professional. The post emphasizes the company's commitment to workplace safety practices but contains no specific information with direct oil market implications.

posts.text: The race for Helium-3 is heating up. While lunar regolith could provide vast future supplies, Earth-based sources remain the only practical option today. Cost, infrastructure, and accessibility continue to give terrestrial production a decisive advantage. #Helium3 #SpaceMining
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Implications

Earth-based helium-3 production currently dominates over lunar alternatives due to superior cost-effectiveness and existing infrastructure, suggesting terrestrial sources will remain the primary supply for the foreseeable future. This implies stable, predictable helium-3 availability from established terrestrial operations rather than disruption from space-based mining ventures.

posts.text: Brent crude rebounded above $80/bbl after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt fighting in southern Lebanon, easing fears that renewed conflict could derail the fragile U.S.-Iran peace process and delay the return of disrupted Middle East oil supplies. #OilMarkets #MiddleEast
posts.summary: Brent crude oil rebounded above $80 per barrel following a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, which reduced concerns about potential disruptions to Middle Eastern oil supplies and the U.S.-Iran peace process. The de-escalation eased market fears about supply disruptions from renewed regional conflict.

posts.text: BP is weighing a sale of its UK upstream portfolio for up to $2.7 billion as it pivots toward higher-growth regions. The move would support its $20 billion divestment target while exploration successes in Brazil, Namibia, Egypt and Angola reshape its future. #BP #OilAndGas
posts.summary: BP is divesting its UK upstream assets for approximately $2.7 billion as part of a broader $20 billion divestment program, while redirecting capital toward exploration opportunities in higher-growth regions including Brazil, Namibia, Egypt, and Angola. This strategic shift reflects changing investment priorities within the oil sector toward emerging markets with significant discovery potential.

posts.text:
U.S. oil and gas rig count edged up one rig to 563 for the week ending June 18 according to Baker Hughes data, with the Niobrara (+1) the week's sole basin mover as all other basins held steady.
https://t.co/vF0rNysWRy
posts.summary: U.S. oil and gas rig count increased marginally to 563 for the week ending June 18, driven solely by a single rig addition in the Niobrara basin while all other major producing regions remained unchanged. The minimal activity suggests relatively flat drilling momentum across the broader U.S. oil and gas sector.

posts.text: Russia is rationing gasoline in Moscow after Ukrainian drone strikes knocked out key refining capacity. The Moscow refinery, which supplies up to 40% of the capital’s fuel, was hit as outages removed an estimated 600,000 bpd of refining capacity. #Russia #Oil #Refining
posts.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes have disabled Russia's Moscow refinery, which typically supplies 40% of the capital's fuel, resulting in gasoline rationing and removing approximately 600,000 barrels per day of refining capacity from the market.

posts.text: #Highlights: 20th Edition of the #OPEC World Oil Outlook (2026) launched via livestream on 18 June 2026 in Vienna, Austria. https://t.co/4gVQElPSYD
posts.summary: OPEC released the 20th edition of its World Oil Outlook report in June 2026, providing updated projections and analysis on global oil market trends. The outlook represents OPEC's official forecast for the oil sector and market conditions ahead.

posts.text:
Our bal-summer Henry Hub forecast sits above the forward curve, but the bigger call is on the ceiling. Withlittle new LNG capacity due online before H2 2027, sustained prices above $3.50/MMBtu outside winter look unlikely.

Strong production growth and a comfortable 3.91 tcf https://t.co/vrUiyoo2XH
posts.summary: Energy Aspects forecasts natural gas prices will remain capped below $3.50/MMBtu outside of winter due to limited new LNG export capacity additions before late 2027, despite strong domestic production growth. The implications for oil markets are not addressed in this post, which focuses specifically on natural gas supply and pricing dynamics.

posts.text: Juneteenth is a federal holiday marking June 19, 1865, when enslaved people in Texas were informed the Civil War had ended and they were free. posts.summary: Marathon Petroleum acknowledged Juneteenth as a federal holiday and stated the company's commitment to reflecting on African American achievement and fostering an inclusive workplace culture. The post contains no information with direct implications for oil markets.
